name: DO-HEALTH omega-3 showed small DNA-methylation clock slowing
summary: DO-HEALTH adds a larger controlled older-adult signal, but the clock effects were small and partly intervention-specific.
statement: In the DO-HEALTH Bio-Age subgroup, omega-3 slowed several DNA-methylation clock measures over three years, with an additive PhenoAge signal for omega-3, vitamin D, and exercise.
population_or_model: Older adults in the Swiss DO-HEALTH Bio-Age subgroup
quantitative_note: The abstract reports standardized effects of 0.16 to 0.32 units, corresponding to about 2.9 to 3.8 months over three years.
caveats: The analysis was post hoc and limited to a subgroup.
Effects were small and not uniform across all clocks and intervention arms.
The endpoint is methylation-clock movement, not proven clinical aging modification.
Clock-related competing interests were disclosed for one author.
